description: Dual-event market: will Russia-Ukraine reach a ceasefire before GTA VI launches in the US? At 55% with $1.4M volume, essentially a coin flip. Functions as a pop-culture benchmark for geopolitical timeline expectations. 50-50 resolution if neither occurs by July 31.
question: Will Russia and Ukraine reach a ceasefire before GTA VI is released?

resolution_logic.summary: Resolves Yes if Russia-Ukraine ceasefire is officially announced before GTA VI is publicly released in the US. Resolves No if GTA VI releases first. Resolves 50-50 if neither occurs by July 31, 2026.

triggers.description: Russia-Ukraine official ceasefire announced before GTA VI US release
triggers.source: Russian/Ukrainian governments for ceasefire; Rockstar Games/Take-Two Interactive for game release

exclusions: Early access, beta versions, pre-release availability, or leaks do not count as GTA VI release
Partial ceasefires (energy infrastructure, Black Sea only)
resolution_logic.ambiguity_notes: Console-only release counts (e.g., Xbox Series X/S only). 50-50 resolution if neither event occurs by July 31 — creates a deadline risk for both sides. GTA VI was originally slated for Fall 2025, then delayed to May 2026, creating uncertainty about the game release anchor.

catalyst_dates.event: GTA VI expected release window (May 2026 per Take-Two)

resolution_source.name: Russian/Ukrainian governments; Rockstar Games / Take-Two Interactive

cross_platform_link.structural_notes: No equivalent on Kalshi. Pop-culture benchmark markets are unique to Polymarket.

related_markets.notes: Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by June 30 at 15% — if ceasefire happens before June 30, this market resolves Yes (assuming GTA VI hasn't launched)
Russia-Ukraine vs US-Iran ceasefire at 14% — another relative-timing market on the same underlying event
